behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: correctness depends on real git graph/time metadata plus real PR existence state, which crosses subprocess + network boundaries. Lower-level miss-proof: an in-process fake git/PR client can miss failures from real git ancestry/date queries, branch-name mapping, or live PR lookup semantics; no LLM decision is involved, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create real branches in a test repo, make commits with controlled commit dates, ensure branch is ahead of main, and query real PR state (none open). Run the staleness detector end-to-end (including git subprocess calls and PR fetch path) and assert it marks only threshold-exceeding unPRed branches as stale.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: this behavior is at session bootstrap/runtime boundary and depends on real current-branch resolution plus startup wiring/output path. Lower-level miss-proof: module-level tests can pass while startup hook/command invocation, environment wiring, or output emission fails in real execution; no AI model output is tested, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start a real session bootstrap entrypoint on a repo state preconditioned as stale, then assert stderr/stdout/log output includes the stale warning and branch context fields (branch name, age, ahead/no-PR signal).

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: behavior explicitly targets a local deterministic mapping function over already-computed state. Lower-level miss-proof: there is no module handoff/I-O boundary in scope; raising to Integration/System would not add failure modes for this specific mapping logic.
behaviors.test_description: Table-drive inputs of computed branch-state structs into the classifier and assert exact label outputs (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) and deterministic repeatability.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: behavior text requires live repo and live PR state in rendered command output, which is real subprocess + real external API integration. Lower-level miss-proof: mocked repo/PR adapters can hide real command wiring, auth/rate-limit/state-shape, and rendering-path failures that only appear end-to-end; no LLM judgment is involved, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke `/kaizen-wip` in a real repo for two scenarios (clean branch, ahead-without-PR stale branch) while reading live PR state, then assert rendered output strings classify each scenario correctly.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: failure boundary is branch-to-PR linkage with real PR lifecycle states (open/merged), typically fetched from external provider plus git branch identity. Lower-level miss-proof: stubs often simplify PR state transitions and branch association rules, hiding false positives caused by real API fields/state timing; no AI/ML output quality is involved, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Use real branches mapped to one open PR and one merged PR, run stale detection, and assert neither branch is labeled `stale-no-pr` even when branch age/commit count would otherwise qualify.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: this is a real git-history transformation case (rebase/squash) where subprocess git behavior and ancestry resolution are the core risk. Lower-level miss-proof: mocked commit graphs can miss edge cases in how real git rewrites SHAs and computes reachability after squash/rebase; no model decision quality is part of correctness, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, execute rebase and squash-merge scenarios that rewrite commit hashes, then run staleness detection and assert branches now represented in main (despite SHA changes) are not classified as stale-no-PR.
